text: Corr 06.25.2026 v26.2 Conflict of Interest Policy Updates are noted in red Conflict of Interest Policy Reference the applicable policy for additional information: • FHA Handbook 4000.1 • Real Estate Settlement Procedures Act (RESPA) Mortgage loan transactions where there is a relationship between a Newrez Family of Company (“Company”) employee(s) and/or the parties to the transaction may cause an increased risk. As such, this Policy outlines the Company’s position regarding conflicts of interest and the procedure necessary to mitigate the risks associated with such relationships. Conflict of Interest arises whenever an employee’s, contractor’s, or agent’s personal interests diverge from their responsibilities to or from the Company’s best interests. In other words, Company employees are expected to avoid activities, investments, or associations that might interfere with their independent exercise of judgment in the company’s best interest. Broker, Broker employee, Broker family member, Correspondent clients, or any party identified by an Affiliated Business Arrangement are also considered a participant within the transaction. Examples include, but are not limited to: • A Loan Consultant, Broker or Client who is a relative of any participant in the transaction. • A borrower who is a relative of any participant in the transaction. • A borrower who is a Company employee. • For all transactions, the borrower cannot act in dual capacity (for example, borrower acting as broker/lender/appraiser/title company in the transaction). The borrower may act as the realtor as long as there is no other business affiliation. • For all transactions, the correspondent/loan officer cannot act in dual capacity (for example, correspondent/loan officer and realtor or correspondent/loan officer and any other affiliation within the transaction). FHA Transactions For FHA transactions, participants that have a direct impact on the mortgage approval decision are

pacity (for example, correspondent/loan officer and realtor or correspondent/loan officer and any other affiliation within the transaction). FHA Transactions For FHA transactions, participants that have a direct impact on the mortgage approval decision are prohibited from having multiple roles or sources of compensation, either directly or indirectly, from a single FHA-insured transaction. These participants are: • Underwriters • Appraisers • Inspectors • Engineers Corr 06.25.2026 v26.2 Conflict of Interest Policy Updates are noted in red Indirect compensation includes any compensation resulting from the same FHA-insured transaction, other than for services performed in a direct role. Examples include, but are not limited to: • Compensation resulting from an ownership interest in any other business that is a party to the same FHA-insured transaction; or • Compensation earned by a spouse, domestic partner, or other family member that has a direct role in the same FHA-insured transaction. The Company must ensure that participants with a direct impact on the mortgage approval decision fs not have multiple roles or sources of compensation from the same FHA-insured transaction. Conflict of Interest Exists If a conflict of interest exists, additional due diligence is required, and the following requirements apply: • Any transaction where the borrower, loan consultant, Broker or Client is determined to have acted in a dual capacity is not permitted. • For any other situation involving conflict of interest, the file is to be assigned to an underwriter that is not related to any party associated with the loan, or else is to be assigned to a Consumer Direct team member for originating, processing, and underwriting. If state licensing is an issue or product type is limited to an area, the underwriting of the file must be assigned by the National UW Manager to the applicable team. • The loan consultant, Broker or Client must identify the conflict, notating within the file that the

ensing is an issue or product type is limited to an area, the underwriting of the file must be assigned by the National UW Manager to the applicable team. • The loan consultant, Broker or Client must identify the conflict, notating within the file that the transaction contains a conflict of interest and nature of the conflict. As a reminder, for loans that have Non-Arm’s Length or Identity of Interest characteristics, refer to the applicable Product Summaries for further detail and eligibility. Revision History (2025) Date • Updated the following as an overlay: For all transactions, the broker/loan officer cannot act in dual capacity (for example, broker/loan officer and realtor or broker/loan officer and any other affiliation within the transaction). 02.26.2026 Updated policy to include WHL 06.25.2026
